what_it_does: Documents an architectural fact: there are three separate systems all called "channels," describes each, and flags an unresolved seam about which channel system's membership should govern dragnet access.
observations: Lists three distinct "channel" systems with separate stores and purposes
The live-messaging transport is a flat handle list for push delivery
The rich work-group system coordinates agents with awake/listening/coordinator/conducted/composed liveness semantics
The cross-boundary external-database system uses a closed-by-default "shared" flag to control outside visibility
The three systems deliberately mirror each other's shapes
Dragnet receipts currently attach to a channel by name, so they could point at any of the three systems
No authority is yet designated for "members who can read this dragnet"
File is a noticeboard block item, part_of board://item-389c8489, in the dragnet-development channel
standouts: The three-channel taxonomy and the warning not to treat them as one thing
The unresolved seam between dragnet receipt attachment and member-gated access
The closed-by-default "shared" flag as the external boundary control
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated, and history
Uses board:// URI addressing and a part_of link
Markdown body uses numbered list, bold labels, and explicit "why this matters" framing
Filed as a block-level noticeboard item
concerns: Identifies an unresolved architectural seam but does not propose a resolution or decision criteria
Assumes prior reader familiarity with dragnet receipts, attachment machinery, and the three channel systems
notes: A conceptual-warning noticeboard item that maps three overloaded "channel" concepts and points out a membership-governance seam for dragnets.
questions: What are the exact system names or identifiers for each of the three channel systems?
What is the existing dragnet attachment machinery and where are dragnet receipts stored?
What is board://item-389c8489 and how does this item relate to it?
How do member lists map or synchronize across the three channel systems, if at all?
What does "conducted mode" and "composed liveness" specifically mean in the rich work-group system?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is to surface the three-channel fact and explain why it matters for dragnet member access, and the body does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Naming collision as an architectural risk
Separation of transport, coordination, and boundary concerns
intention_signals: To make a hidden 'one word, three systems' ambiguity explicit before the team builds member-gated dragnet access, and to push for a decision about which channel system owns the membership rule.
novelty: It treats the collision of three "channel" concepts as a first-class architectural fact rather than a naming accident, and frames the dragnet-channel attachment as an underspecified seam.
contribution: Keeps the three-channel taxonomy and the unresolved dragnet-membership seam visible as an explicit decision point for access-rule design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item explaining that the company has three separate systems called "channels"—a live-messaging transport, a rich work-group coordinator, and a closed-by-default cross-boundary store—and flagging an unresolved seam about which system's membership should govern dragnet access.
intention_for_embedding: To surface a hidden "one name, three systems" architectural ambiguity before designing member-gated dragnet access.
